summary: First end-to-end test for Screena digital signage CMS. Backend: 24/24 pytest cases pass (auth/jwt cookies, playlists CRUD + owner isolation, screens CRUD, public /api/play/{code}, PPTX import generating editable blocks, weather proxy, health). Frontend: critical flows verified - admin login, register new user, dashboard, editor with all 7 block types (text/image/video/shape/countdown/clock/weather) draggable via react-rnd, layers panel, slide thumbnails, save, screens page with pair_code generation, playlist assignment, public player rendering, /preview/:id. Found one HIGH bug: copyLink throws an uncaught NotAllowedError because navigator.clipboard.writeText() is not wrapped in try/catch and clipboard write is denied in this cross-origin iframe environment - this surfaces as a full-screen runtime-error overlay to the user.

ui_bugs.issue: navigator.clipboard.writeText() is not wrapped in try/catch. In environments where Clipboard API is denied (cross-origin iframes, insecure contexts, browsers without permission), it throws an unhandled promise rejection 'Failed to execute writeText on Clipboard: Write permission denied' which renders the React error overlay on top of the whole app. Reproduced by clicking the copy-link button on a screen card in this preview environment.

Uses browser-native window.prompt() and window.confirm() for screen name and delete confirmation. Works functionally but is inconsistent with the rest of the app (which uses Sonner toasts and custom UI). Recommend a Dialog/AlertDialog modal.

React hydration warning: <span> cannot be a child of <option> (likely caused by dev instrumentation wrapping dynamic text in a span). This is logged as a console error on the Screens page. Likely not impacting prod but noisy in console.

action_items: Wrap navigator.clipboard.writeText() in try/catch (or check navigator.clipboard availability first) and fall back to a hidden <textarea> + document.execCommand('copy'), then show toast.error on failure instead of letting the promise reject. This affects /app/frontend/src/pages/Screens.jsx copyLink().
Replace native prompt()/confirm() in Screens.jsx with a Dialog component for consistency and to make the flows mobile/keyboard friendly (optional improvement).
(Optional) Investigate the <span> inside <option> hydration warning - check if it stems from app code or external instrumentation; if app code, render the playlist name directly without wrapper.
critical_code_review_comments: Auth: bcrypt password hashing in /app/backend/auth.py uses correct $2b$ format; httpOnly access_token + refresh_token cookies are set with secure=True and samesite='none' which is required for cross-site cookie usage; CORS is allow_origin_regex='.*' + allow_credentials=True (works for preview but consider explicit allowed origins for production).
MongoDB: routes return Pydantic objects, no raw _id leakage observed in any tested endpoint (auth/me, playlists, screens, play).
Public endpoint /api/play/{pair_code} correctly does NOT require auth and updates last_seen heartbeat.
Editor: pixel coordinates at 1920x1080 with CSS transform scale - clean implementation; react-rnd 'scale' prop used correctly so drag deltas remain in slide pixel space.
PPTX import correctly parses text/shape blocks and converts EMU to pixels; rejects non-pptx with 400.
Owner isolation enforced in playlists endpoints - user B cannot see/access user A's playlists (verified via test).
Brute-force lockout exists per ip:email (15 min after 5 fails) - good. Tests use unique emails to avoid tripping it.
Editor.jsx is ~700 lines (acceptable). Consider splitting Inspector and Toolbar into separate files for maintainability.

success_rate.frontend: ~92% - all critical flows work; one HIGH bug in copy-link clipboard handling

test_credentials: Seeded admin admin@screena.app / admin123 (from /app/memory/test_credentials.md) - works. Newly registered E2E users created during tests are TEST_-prefixed or e2e_<timestamp>@example.com.
seed_data_creation: Backend tests create TEST_-prefixed playlists/screens and e2e timestamped users; not cleaned up post-run (acceptable for first iteration, can add teardown fixture later).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend tests are at /app/backend/tests/test_screena_api.py (run: pytest backend/tests/test_screena_api.py -v). They generate a sample PPTX at runtime via python-pptx. Admin password is admin123. On the Frontend, the Screens page uses native prompt() so Playwright dialog handlers must be attached BEFORE clicking add-screen-button. The clipboard copyLink bug is reproducible by clicking [data-testid^='copy-link-'] - the React error overlay covers the page.
rca of the issue: Clipboard error RCA: in /app/frontend/src/pages/Screens.jsx line 42-46, copyLink() awaits navigator.clipboard.writeText(url) without try/catch. The browser rejects writeText in this cross-origin iframe sandbox preview environment, the promise rejection bubbles up, and Create React App's dev error overlay (react-error-overlay) renders on top of the page. Fix: const safeCopy = async (text) => { try { await navigator.clipboard?.writeText(text); toast.success('Public URL copied'); } catch { /* fallback to execCommand or just toast.error('Copy failed'); */ } }. The same pattern should be applied wherever clipboard is used in the codebase.
